top_signals.title: Japan 2-Year Bond Sale Sees Demand In Line With 12-Month Average
top_signals.summary: Japan’s two-year government bond auction drew demand that was in line with its 12-month average as elevated yields attracted investors despite uncertainty surrounding the war in the Middle East.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Iron Ore Heads for Monthly Loss as Coal Spike Pressures Margins
top_signals.summary: Iron ore headed for a monthly loss as a short-lived rally triggered by a fatal steelmaking-coal mine accident in China’s Shanxi province faded, leaving investors focused on mill margins and mounting shipments.

alerts.title: Burnham hits back at Blair with more state control for ‘good growth’
alerts.summary: Andy Burnham has called for stronger state intervention in the economy to deliver what he calls “good growth,” lashing back at Tony Blair’s blueprint for Labour’s future direction. The Greater Manchester mayor conceded areas of agreement with the three-time election winner — notably on the importance of economic growth— but accused Blair of a “gaping [...]

alerts.title: Summer Heatwaves May Deepen the Global LNG Crunch
alerts.summary: The global gas market, which has been reeling from the sudden loss of 20% of daily LNG supply, is poised to tighten even further in the coming months as higher-than-expected summer temperatures and the El Niño weather pattern are expected to raise gas demand in Asia.  Asian and European gas prices could rally even higher if the Strait of Hormuz remains inaccessible to most LNG tankers this summer, while Asia struggles to meet cooling demand and Europe looks to refill gas storage sites that have depleted to multi-year lows at the end…
